title: Repository Repair, Release, and Collaboration Capstone · Git Stakeholder Mission
summary: A small engineering team needs a safe contribution, review, release, and recovery workflow. Deliver a tested workflow and resolve a changed repository incident under collaboration constraints. Planned artifact: Deliver Team Repository Operations Portfolio with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.
Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — A project repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v4.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.

content.text: A project repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v4.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

steps.title: Handoff Runbook · changed case
steps.instruction:
project case — A project repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

Which action demonstrates Handoff Runbook without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.

content.text: A project repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Handoff Runbook condition decided your prediction.

content.value: gitrevisions Manual — Controls revision names, ancestry, range, exclusion, reflog, and object selection semantics.

options.text: For handoff runbook · changed case, apply Handoff Runbook without its stated preconditions, omit the a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Handoff Runbook for a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio.

Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio.
Experienced contributors do not need repository-specific recovery documentation. This does not resolve handoff runbook · changed case.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.
